description: User Profile Menu & Logout - Display Google profile avatar in top-right corner with dropdown menu showing name, email, and logout action across all authenticated pages

userStories.title: Create UserProfileMenu component with avatar display
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable Svelte component that displays the user's Google profile picture as a small circle and renders a fallback avatar when no picture is available.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/components/UserProfileMenu.svelte that accepts name, email, and picture props
Display Google profile picture as a 36px circle using border-radius: 50% and object-fit: cover
Add referrerpolicy='no-referrer' to the img element for Google-hosted images
If picture prop is empty or null, render a fallback circle with the first letter of the user's name
Fallback uses --ci-accent background with white text, matching the app's design system
Avatar has a subtle border/ring on hover to indicate it is clickable (cursor: pointer)
Component uses existing CSS variables (--ci-surface, --ci-text, --ci-border, --ci-accent) for light/dark theme support
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the base component. The dropdown menu is added in US-002. Keep the component self-contained — it will be placed in page headers in later stories.

userStories.title: Add dropdown menu with user info and logout option
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click my profile picture to see my account details and a logout option so I can manage my session.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the avatar toggles a dropdown menu below the avatar, aligned to the right edge
Dropdown displays the user's Google profile picture (larger, ~48px), full name, and email address
Dropdown includes a 'Sign out' button styled with existing .ws-btn or similar design system classes
Dropdown closes when clicking outside of it (use window click listener with stopPropagation on the menu)
Dropdown closes when pressing Escape key
Dropdown has subtle box-shadow and border consistent with .ws-card styling
Dropdown does not overflow the viewport on mobile (right-aligned with minimum right: 8px from viewport edge)
The 'Sign out' button dispatches a 'logout' event from the component (actual logout logic handled by parent)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The component dispatches a 'logout' event rather than calling the API directly — this keeps the component reusable and lets each page handle logout + cleanup as needed.

userStories.title: Add UserProfileMenu to interview page header
userStories.description: As a student on the interview page, I want to see my profile picture in the top-right corner so I know I'm signed in and can log out.
acceptanceCriteria: UserProfileMenu is rendered in the top-right corner of the interview page header (.ws-header)
User data (name, email, picture) is passed from the existing data.user prop provided by +page.server.js
Avatar is positioned with position: absolute, right: 24px, top: 50%, transform: translateY(-50%) relative to the header so it does not disturb the centered layout
On the 'logout' event: POST to /.netlify/functions/auth-logout, clear ws_* localStorage keys, then redirect to /login using window.location.href
Profile menu is visible on all interview sub-screens (entry, rooms, waiting, interview, debrief)
On mobile viewports (<=600px), avatar uses right: 16px and dropdown stays within viewport bounds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The interview page header uses .ws-header with centered text. The avatar must be absolutely positioned so it floats in the top-right without pushing the centered title. The header container needs position: relative if it doesn't already have it.

userStories.title: Add UserProfileMenu to dashboard page header
userStories.description: As an instructor on the dashboard, I want to see my profile picture and be able to log out.
acceptanceCriteria: UserProfileMenu is rendered in the top-right corner of the dashboard page header (.ws-header--wide)
User data is fetched from /.netlify/functions/auth-session on dashboard mount (dashboard has its own auth check)
Avatar is positioned the same way as on the interview page (absolute, right-aligned in header)
On the 'logout' event: POST to /.netlify/functions/auth-logout, clear sessionStorage dashboard auth, then redirect to /login
Profile menu does not break the existing dashboard header layout or the 'Copy Join Link' button
On mobile viewports, avatar uses right: 16px and dropdown stays within viewport bounds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The dashboard currently uses sessionStorage for its own password-based auth. Logout should clear both the session cookie (via auth-logout) and any sessionStorage keys used by the dashboard.
